texto: If Jesus was put to death by powerful elites protecting their turf, then the murder is only a historical or academic puzzle. But if those who knew Him best—His longtime admirers—were involved, everything changes. It suddenly becomes personal, for we are then forced to confront the more troubling questions: Would I have sided with the majority and cried out for His blood? Would you?
explicacao: Pergunta retórica de Timothy J. Stoner que provoca passagem do tempo e prioridades. Ao questionar “If Jesus was put to death by powerful elites protecting their turf, then the murder is only a his…”, o texto convida o leitor a revisar crenças sobre tempo, christianity, jesus, com foco em luto e mudanca.

frase_original: If Jesus was put to death by powerful elites protecting their turf, then the murder is only a historical or academic puzzle. But if those who knew Him best—His longtime admirers—were involved, everything changes. It suddenly becomes personal, for we are then forced to confront the more troubling questions: Would I have sided with the majority and cried out for His blood? Would you?

texto: By shattering my preconceptions and cultural stereotypes, what materialized was a massive personality who was astonishingly winsome yet gravely terrifying. A man whose breathtaking compassion would draw you in, but whose ferocious honesty could cut and slice like a razor. Someone who was the most controversial, confrontational, divisive, yet compellingly beautiful man who has ever lived. Which is precisely what we would expect if the Creator of the universe happened to invade our world.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Timothy J. Stoner: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de esforço, persistência e resultados. O trecho “By shattering my preconceptions and cultural stereotypes, what materialized was a massive persona…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que sucesso, jesus, jesus-christ pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de jesus-christ e new-testament.
